subject: english
name_zh: IB DP · 英语语言与文学 A
name_en: IB DP English A: Language and Literature
description: IB DP English A: Literature and Language & Literature courses. 7 concepts (Identity, Culture, Creativity, Communication, Perspective, Transformation, Representation). 3 areas of exploration.

domains.name: 读者-作者-文本 / Readers, Writers, Texts / Readers, Writers, Texts

nodes.name: 文本细读
curriculum_points: Students will be able to perform a detailed, line-by-line analysis of a literary or non-literary text, identifying and interpreting the effects of specific word choices, imagery, and syntax.
Students will be able to construct a coherent interpretation of a text's local meaning and overall significance based on sustained attention to textual detail.

nodes.name: 文学分析
curriculum_points: Students will be able to formulate a clear, arguable thesis about a literary work's meaning, supported by well-chosen textual evidence and logical reasoning.
Students will be able to evaluate how various literary elements (e.g., character, setting, theme, structure) interact to shape a work's overall impact and meaning.

nodes.name: 叙事技巧
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse and evaluate the function of narrative point of view, voice, focalization, and temporal structure in shaping meaning and reader response in prose fiction.
Students will be able to explain how authors use techniques such as characterization, dialogue, and plot development to construct narrative and convey thematic concerns.

nodes.name: 诗歌分析
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse the relationship between form (e.g., sonnet, free verse, stanzaic structure) and content in a poem, explaining how poetic techniques (meter, rhyme, enjambment) contribute to its meaning and effect.
Students will be able to interpret complex imagery, metaphor, and symbolism in poetry, situating their analysis within the poem's broader thematic and emotional landscape.

nodes.name: 非文学文本
curriculum_points: Students will be able to apply principles of textual analysis to a range of non-literary text types (e.g., advertisements, opinion columns, speeches, infographics), identifying their purpose, audience, and contextual constraints.
Students will be able to deconstruct the use of visual and linguistic design elements in non-literary texts to understand how they construct meaning and position the audience.

domains.name: 时间与空间 / Time and Space / Time & Space

nodes.name: 创作语境
curriculum_points: Students will be able to research and explain how the circumstances of a text's creation (author's background, historical events, publishing milieu, technological means) influence its form, content, and potential meanings.

nodes.name: 接受语境
curriculum_points: Students will be able to evaluate how different audiences, across time and culture, might receive and interpret a text differently, considering factors like ideology, social norms, and medium of consumption.

nodes.name: 文化语境
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how texts reflect, represent, or challenge the values, beliefs, and social practices of specific cultures or subcultures.
Students will be able to explore the role of texts in shaping cultural identity and negotiating cultural power dynamics.

nodes.name: 历史背景
curriculum_points: Students will be able to situate a text within its specific historical period, drawing connections between its themes, style, or concerns and broader historical developments or intellectual movements of the era.

domains.name: 互文与跨文本 / Intertextuality / Intertextuality

nodes.name: 文体传统
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify the defining formal and thematic conventions of major literary genres (e.g., tragedy, satire, bildungsroman) and analyse how individual texts adhere to or subvert these conventions.
Students will be able to classify non-literary texts by genre (e.g., editorial, manifesto, travel writing) and evaluate the strategic use of generic conventions to achieve a communicative purpose.

nodes.name: 比较研究
curriculum_points: Students will be able to construct a focused, insightful comparison of two or more texts, moving beyond superficial similarities/differences to explore deeper connections in theme, technique, or context.
Students will be able to use comparison as a critical tool to illuminate the distinctive features and broader significance of each text in the pair or group.

nodes.name: 文学流派
curriculum_points: Students will be able to define key characteristics of major literary movements (e.g., Romanticism, Modernism, Postcolonialism) and analyse how specific texts exemplify the aesthetic and philosophical principles of a movement.

domains.name: 评估任务 / Assessment Components / Assessment

nodes.name: IA 个人口头
curriculum_points: Students will be able to deliver a structured, 10-minute oral presentation that analyses the relationship between a global issue and the content and form of one literary and one non-literary text.
Students will be able to engage in a thoughtful, spontaneous discussion with the teacher, demonstrating the ability to expand upon and defend their analysis in response to probing questions.

nodes.name: Paper 1 · 引导分析
curriculum_points: Students will be able to write a coherent, analytical essay in response to an unseen literary or non-literary text, guided by a provided prompt, within a timed examination setting.
Students will be able to select and analyse salient features of the text (both linguistic and stylistic), building a sustained argument that addresses the guiding question.

nodes.name: Paper 2 · 比较小论文
curriculum_points: Students will be able to write a comparative essay, under timed conditions, in response to one of four general questions, drawing on their knowledge of at least two literary works studied in the course.
Students will be able to structure an essay that integrates comparison throughout, using relevant evidence from both works to develop a persuasive response to the chosen question.

nodes.name: HL 论文（4000词）
curriculum_points: Students will be able to produce a formal, 1200-1500 word academic essay that develops a clear line of inquiry into a literary or non-literary text(s) studied in the course, demonstrating independent research and critical thinking.

Students will be able to construct a sophisticated, sustained argument, supported by precise textual evidence and engagement with relevant scholarly sources, and presented with appropriate academic conventions.

domains.name: 语言与表达 / Language in Use / Language in Use

nodes.name: 修辞手法
curriculum_points: Students will be able to identify and analyse the strategic use of rhetorical devices (e.g., anaphora, antithesis, rhetorical questions) in both literary and non-literary texts, explaining their effect on the audience and contribution to the author's purpose.

nodes.name: 语域与语气
curriculum_points: Students will be able to analyse how variations in register (levels of formality, technicality) and tone (e.g., ironic, solemn, polemical) are constructed through diction, syntax, and modality to position the reader and shape meaning.

nodes.name: 说服与修辞
curriculum_points: Students will be able to deconstruct the persuasive strategies employed in argumentative texts, evaluating the use of logical appeals (logos), emotional appeals (pathos), and ethical appeals (ethos).
Students will be able to analyse how texts seek to influence audience beliefs, attitudes, or behaviours, considering the interplay of argument, style, and context.
